title: Canada decriminalizes same-sex acts between adults
description: The Criminal Law Amendment Act removed criminal penalties for private homosexual acts between consenting adults aged 21 and over. It passed on 14 May 1969 and received royal assent on 27 June 1969.

title: First national gay liberation protest at Parliament Hill
description: About 100 people gathered on Parliament Hill in Ottawa for Canada's first organized gay liberation demonstration, with a smaller simultaneous protest held in Vancouver.

title: Pride Week '73 marked across Canadian cities
description: Coordinated Pride Week events took place in Vancouver, Toronto, Ottawa, Montreal, Saskatoon and Winnipeg, marking an early nationwide expression of gay and lesbian visibility.

title: Early Vancouver Pride events held in Ceperley Park
description: The Gay Alliance Toward Equality organized some of Vancouver's earliest Pride celebrations, gathering at Ceperley Park in Stanley Park.

title: Homosexuality removed from the DSM disorder list
description: The American Psychiatric Association voted to remove homosexuality as a disorder from its Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, a decision widely referenced across North American LGBTQ history.

title: The Brunswick Four arrested at a Toronto tavern
description: Four women were arrested at the Brunswick Tavern in Toronto after singing a satirical song and refusing to leave, a case that became a rallying point for lesbian rights.

title: Protest against pre-Olympic Montreal police raids
description: More than 300 people rallied in Montreal against a wave of police raids on bathhouses and gay bars conducted in the lead-up to the 1976 Summer Olympics.

title: Nova Scotia's first public LGBT protest in Halifax
description: Twenty-one demonstrators picketed CBC headquarters in Halifax, staging what is recorded as Nova Scotia's first public LGBT protest.

title: First nationally coordinated gay demonstrations
description: Gay and lesbian demonstrations were held simultaneously in Montreal, Ottawa, Toronto, Winnipeg and Vancouver, the first time such protests were coordinated nationally.

title: York Rainbow Society of the Deaf established
description: Raymond Barton, Bonnie Perry and Randy Vivian started the York Rainbow Society of the Deaf in the Toronto area, recorded as the first queer Deaf group in Canada.

title: Montreal police raid the Truxx and Le Mystique bars
description: Police raided the Truxx and Le Mystique gay bars in Montreal and arrested 146 men. The following day around 2,000 people took to the streets in protest.

title: Quebec adds sexual orientation to its rights code
description: Quebec amended its Charter of Human Rights and Freedoms to prohibit discrimination based on sexual orientation, becoming the first province in Canada to do so.

title: Buddies in Bad Times theatre founded in Toronto
description: The Buddies in Bad Times theatre company began operating in Toronto, growing into one of the largest queer theatres in the world.

title: Immigration ban on homosexuals lifted in Canada
description: The Immigration Act of 1976 came into force, removing the provision that had barred homosexuals from immigrating to Canada.

title: Montreal holds its first gay and lesbian pride march
description: Fifty-two people took part in Montreal's first gay and lesbian pride march.

title: Second queer Deaf group formed in Montreal
description: L'Association des Bonnes Gens Sourds was formed in Montreal, recorded as the second queer Deaf group in Canada.

title: Operation Soap bathhouse raids in Toronto
description: In Operation Soap, Toronto police raided four bathhouses and arrested roughly 300 men. The mass arrests sparked large protests and are seen as a turning point for the Canadian gay rights movement.

title: First lesbian pride march in Canada held in Vancouver
description: More than 200 people joined the first lesbian pride march in Canada, held in Vancouver.

title: Pisces bathhouse raid in Edmonton
description: Police raided the Pisces Health Spa bathhouse in Edmonton and arrested 56 men.

title: Vancouver holds its first official Pride Parade
description: Vancouver's first official Pride Parade drew more than 1,500 participants.

title: Dykes in the Streets march held in Toronto
description: Lesbians Against the Right organized the Dykes in the Streets march in Toronto, drawing around 350 participants.

title: Glad Day Bookshop raided by Toronto police
description: Toronto police raided the Glad Day Bookshop, one of several actions targeting the store over its LGBTQ material.

title: Edmonton holds its first Gay Pride Weekend
description: Edmonton's first Gay Pride Weekend was held at Camp Harris over several days.

title: Toronto schools adopt anti-gay discrimination program
description: The Toronto District School Board put in place one of Canada's first programs aimed at combating anti-gay discrimination in schools.

title: Parliament releases the Equality for All report
description: The Parliamentary Committee on Equality Rights released its Equality for All report, which recommended protections against discrimination based on sexual orientation.

title: Canada Customs seizes books bound for Little Sister's
description: Canada Customs seized more than 500 books and magazines destined for Little Sister's Book and Art Emporium in Vancouver, part of a long-running censorship dispute.

title: Winnipeg holds its first official Pride Week
description: Winnipeg marked its first official Pride Week, drawing around 250 attendees.

title: Svend Robinson becomes Canada's first openly gay MP
description: Member of Parliament Svend Robinson publicly came out, becoming the first openly gay MP in Canada.

title: Halifax holds its first official Pride March
description: Halifax's first official Pride March drew about 75 participants.

title: WHO removes homosexuality from disease classification
description: The World Health Organization voted to remove homosexuality from its International Classification of Diseases, a decision now commemorated as the origin of the International Day Against Homophobia.

title: The term Two-Spirit coined in Winnipeg
description: The English term Two-Spirit, from the Anishinaabe niizh manidoowag, was adopted at the third annual Native American and First Nations Gay and Lesbian Conference in Winnipeg.

title: Sex Garage raid sparks clashes in Montreal
description: A police raid on the Sex Garage loft party in Montreal set off roughly 36 hours of confrontations between police and demonstrators, galvanizing the city's queer activism.

title: Little Sister's challenges Customs censorship powers
description: Little Sister's bookstore launched a constitutional challenge against Canada Customs over its power to censor imported LGBTQ material.

title: Toronto officially endorses Lesbian and Gay Pride Day
description: The City of Toronto formally endorsed Lesbian and Gay Pride Day, giving the event official municipal recognition.

title: Court reads sexual orientation into federal rights law
description: The Ontario Court of Appeal read sexual orientation into the Canadian Human Rights Act as a prohibited ground of discrimination.

title: Ban on gays and lesbians in the military lifted
description: A federal court decision ended the prohibition on gay and lesbian members serving in the Canadian Armed Forces.

title: Supreme Court allows refugee claims based on orientation
description: The Supreme Court of Canada ruled that gay and lesbian people could apply for refugee status on the basis of persecution for their sexual orientation.

title: Montreal's Katakombes bar raided by police
description: Police raided the Katakombes bar in Montreal and arrested 165 men.

title: Prince Edward Island holds its first pride march
description: Prince Edward Island held its first gay and lesbian pride march.

title: Rights commission finds Hamilton mayor discriminated
description: The Ontario Human Rights Commission found that the mayor of Hamilton had discriminated against homosexuals, in a case tied to the refusal to proclaim Pride.

title: Ontario becomes first province to allow adoption
description: Ontario became the first Canadian province to allow same-sex couples to jointly adopt children.

title: Supreme Court reads orientation into the Charter
description: In Egan v. Canada the Supreme Court of Canada ruled that sexual orientation is protected under the equality guarantee of the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms.

title: London Pride refusal later ruled discriminatory
description: The City of London refused a request to proclaim Pride; the refusal was subsequently found to be discriminatory.

title: Bill C-33 adds sexual orientation to federal rights law
description: Parliament passed Bill C-33, adding sexual orientation as a prohibited ground of discrimination in the Canadian Human Rights Act.

title: Counting Past 2 trans arts festival launches in Toronto
description: Mirha-Soleil Ross launched Counting Past 2 in Toronto, described as the first transgender arts festival.

title: Supreme Court rules for protection in Vriend v. Alberta
description: In Vriend v. Alberta the Supreme Court of Canada held that provinces cannot exclude sexual orientation from their human rights protections, reading it into Alberta's legislation.

title: Supreme Court rules for equal treatment in M v. H
description: In M v. H the Supreme Court of Canada held that same-sex couples are entitled to the same treatment as opposite-sex common-law couples, prompting wide legislative change.

title: Blockorama debuts as a Black queer space at Pride
description: Blockorama first appeared at Toronto Pride, establishing a dedicated Black queer space within the festival.

title: Parliament votes to keep opposite-sex marriage definition
description: The federal government voted to preserve the legal definition of marriage as the union of one man and one woman, a setback for marriage equality.

title: Ontario introduces Bill 5 on same-sex couples
description: Bill 5 was introduced in the Ontario legislature to extend rights and obligations to same-sex couples, following the M v. H ruling.

title: Alberta passes Bill 202 on marriage definition
description: Alberta passed Bill 202, signalling it would invoke the notwithstanding clause to resist any redefinition of marriage to include same-sex couples.

title: Bill C-23 extends benefits to same-sex couples
description: Parliament passed Bill C-23, extending the same benefits and obligations available to common-law couples to same-sex couples across federal law.

title: Pussy Palace raid at a Toronto women's bathhouse event
description: Toronto police raided Club Toronto during the Pussy Palace, an event for queer and trans women, prompting complaints and legal action over the intrusion.

title: Supreme Court finds Customs targeted Little Sister's
description: The Supreme Court of Canada ruled that Canada Customs had unfairly targeted the Little Sister's bookstore in its handling of imported LGBTQ material.

title: Vancouver marches after the murder of Aaron Webster
description: More than 3,000 people marched in Vancouver to protest anti-gay violence following the killing of Aaron Webster in Stanley Park.

title: Northwest Territories first to protect gender identity
description: The Northwest Territories became the first jurisdiction in Canada to explicitly add gender identity to its human rights legislation.

title: Court affirms Marc Hall's right to attend prom
description: The Ontario Superior Court ruled that student Marc Hall had the right to bring his boyfriend to his Catholic school prom.

title: Canada extends immigration rights to same-sex partners
description: Canada extended immigration sponsorship rights to same-sex partners.

title: Ontario court rules the marriage ban unconstitutional
description: The Ontario Superior Court ruled that prohibiting same-sex couples from marrying is unconstitutional, a decision central to the Halpern case.

title: Goliath's Sauna and Texas Lounge raided in Calgary
description: Police raided Goliath's Sauna and the Texas Lounge in Calgary, one of the later bathhouse raids in Canadian history.

title: Quebec City holds its first Pride parade
description: Quebec City held its first Pride parade.

title: First legal same-sex marriage in Canada in Toronto
description: Michael Leshner and Michael Stark married in Toronto immediately after the Ontario court ruling, becoming the first same-sex couple to legally marry in Canada.

title: Warehouse raids in Hamilton lead to arrests
description: Authorities raided a Hamilton venue and arrested two patrons on indecency charges in what became known as the Warehouse raids.

title: Bill C-38 legalizes same-sex marriage nationwide
description: Bill C-38 became federal law, giving same-sex couples the legal right to marry across Canada and making the country one of the first in the world to do so.

title: House of Commons rejects reopening marriage debate
description: A motion to reopen the same-sex marriage debate was defeated in the House of Commons by a vote of 175 to 123, affirming marriage equality.

title: Supreme Court dismisses appeal in the Nixon case
description: The Supreme Court of Canada declined to hear the appeal in Kimberly Nixon's case against Vancouver Rape Relief, a decision concerning transgender discrimination.

title: Ontario court rules on same-sex survivor benefits
description: An Ontario court ruled that the government was discriminating against same-sex couples in the provision of survivor benefits.

title: Toronto holds its first Trans March
description: Toronto's first Trans March proceeded from Church and Bloor to Church and Wellesley Streets.

title: Fredericton holds its first official Pride
description: Fredericton's first official Pride drew more than 300 marchers.

title: Winter Olympics host the first Pride House
description: The 2010 Winter Olympics in British Columbia featured a Pride House for the first time, offering a welcoming space for LGBTQ athletes and visitors.

title: Quebec holds its first trans rights protest
description: PolitiQ-queer solidaire organized the first trans rights protest in Quebec, drawing around 200 participants.

title: Angela James enters the Hockey Hall of Fame
description: Angela James was inducted into the Hockey Hall of Fame, becoming its first openly gay inductee.

title: Saskatchewan bars marriage officers from refusing couples
description: Saskatchewan courts ruled that marriage commissioners cannot refuse to marry same-sex couples on religious grounds.

title: Yukon holds its first Pride March
description: Stephanie Hammond and Fiona Griffin organized Yukon's first Pride March.

title: Northwest Territories holds its first Pride events
description: The Northwest Territories held its first Pride events over several days.

title: Kathleen Wynne becomes Canada's first openly LGBT premier
description: Kathleen Wynne was sworn in as Premier of Ontario, becoming the province's first female premier and Canada's first openly LGBT head of a provincial government.

title: Bill C-32 enables divorce for non-resident couples
description: Bill C-32 received royal assent, allowing couples who married in Canada but live elsewhere to divorce here even where their home jurisdiction does not recognize the marriage.

title: Second international asexuality conference in Toronto
description: The second International Asexuality Conference was held at Ryerson University in Toronto.

title: Iqaluit holds its first Pride Festival
description: Iqaluit held Nunavut's first Pride Festival.

title: Alberta elects its first out non-binary politician
description: Estefan Cortes-Vargas was elected to the Alberta legislature, becoming the province's first openly non-binary politician.

title: St. John's holds a Newfoundland trans march
description: A trans march was held in St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, drawing more than 70 marchers.

title: Pride flag raised on Parliament Hill for the first time
description: The Pride flag was raised on Parliament Hill in Ottawa for the first time in Canadian history.

title: Blood donation deferral for MSM cut to one year
description: Health Canada approved reducing the blood donation deferral period for men who have sex with men from five years to one year.

title: Black Lives Matter halts the Toronto Pride parade
description: Black Lives Matter Toronto brought the Pride parade to a halt to demand action on anti-Black racism within the LGBTQ community.

title: Hamilton settles trans washroom access complaint
description: The City of Hamilton settled a human rights complaint brought by a transgender woman who had been denied access to a washroom.

title: Haldimand Norfolk holds its first official Pride
description: The first official Pride in Haldimand Norfolk was held at Dunnville Central Park, drawing an estimated 200 to 300 attendees.

title: Bill C-16 protects gender identity and expression
description: Bill C-16 passed, adding gender identity and gender expression to the Canadian Human Rights Act and the hate-crime provisions of the Criminal Code.

title: Julie Lemieux becomes Canada's first transgender mayor
description: Julie Lemieux was elected mayor of Tres-Saint-Redempteur, Quebec, becoming Canada's first openly transgender mayor.

title: Ontario marks Trans Day of Remembrance in law
description: Bill 74 passed in Ontario, formally marking November 20 as Trans Day of Remembrance in the province.

title: Kael McKenzie becomes Canada's first transgender judge
description: Kael McKenzie was appointed to the Provincial Court of Manitoba, becoming the first openly transgender judge in Canada.

title: Bill C-66 creates expungement for unjust convictions
description: Bill C-66 received royal assent, establishing a process to expunge historically unjust criminal convictions for consensual same-sex activity.

title: Petition filed to end the blood ban for gay men
description: Electronic petition E-1589 was filed, calling on the government to end the blood donation ban affecting gay and bisexual men.

title: Blood donation deferral for MSM cut to three months
description: Health Canada announced that the blood donation deferral period for men who have sex with men would be lowered to three months.

title: WHO removes transgender from mental disorder list
description: The World Health Organization voted to remove being transgender from its classification of mental disorders in the ICD-11.

title: University of Winnipeg opens first Two-Spirit archives
description: The University of Winnipeg launched what is recorded as the first Two-Spirit archives in Canada.

title: Bill C-75 equalizes the age of consent
description: Bill C-75 passed, repealing Section 159 of the Criminal Code and equalizing the age of consent at 16 for all sexual acts.

title: Intersex flag raised at London City Hall
description: The intersex flag was raised at City Hall in London, Ontario to mark Intersex Awareness Day.

title: Protest at Toronto library over transphobic event
description: Hundreds of people protested outside the Toronto Public Library over its hosting of a speaker widely criticized as transphobic.

title: Court affirms a minor's right to hormone treatment
description: The British Columbia Court of Appeal reaffirmed a 15-year-old's right to access hormone treatment without parental consent.

title: Quebec court strikes down civil code trans provisions
description: The Quebec Superior Court declared six provisions of the Civil Code unconstitutional for how they treated transgender and non-binary people.

title: First openly Two-Spirit MP elected in Canada
description: Blake Desjarlais was elected in Edmonton-Griesbach, becoming the first openly Two-Spirit Member of Parliament in Canada.

title: First intersex flag crosswalk painted in Canada
description: Canada's first intersex flag crosswalk was painted at the Central Avenue and Wellington Street intersection in London, Ontario.

title: Bill C-4 criminalizes conversion therapy nationwide
description: Bill C-4 received royal assent, banning conversion therapy across Canada through the Criminal Code.

title: Western University removes an IDAHOT support post
description: Western University deleted a social media post supporting the International Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia and Biphobia following backlash from a religious community.

title: New Brunswick amends Policy 713 on trans students
description: The New Brunswick education minister amended Policy 713 in a way that limited protections for transgender students in schools.

title: Saskatchewan adopts parental consent school policy
description: Saskatchewan introduced new parental inclusion and consent policies affecting transgender students in schools.

title: Conservative Party adopts anti-trans policy resolutions
description: The Conservative Party of Canada adopted a set of gender policy resolutions widely described as discriminatory toward transgender people at its national convention.
